title: Lab 37: Services and Default Target
description: Manage the default target on client and key services on server.

tasks: On client, configure the system to boot into multi-user.target by default.
On server, ensure the rsyslog service is enabled and running on server, and if postfix is installed disable it and stop it.
hints: The default target task belongs on client.
Treat postfix as conditional: only act if it is installed.
checks: # server systemctl is-enabled rsyslog | grep -qx enabled && systemctl is-active rsyslog | grep -qx active && sh -lc 'rpm -q postfix >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; systemctl is-enabled postfix 2>/dev/null | grep -qx disabled'
solution_outline: Set the default target to multi-user.target.
Enable and start rsyslog, then disable postfix only if the package is present.
task_titles: Set the default boot target on client
Manage rsyslog and postfix on server
solution_commands: rpm -q postfix >/dev/null 2>&1 && systemctl disable --now postfix || true
